description: Letter from Yosef b. Yehuda Kohen to Netanel b. [...]. (The place for the sender's father's name is left blank.) In Judaeo-Arabic, with some Hebrew. The sender trusts that the addressee obeyed his order to shake out the cloaks (aksiyā), for now he is supposed to hand them over to the bearer of the letter, Mūsā b. Yūsuf along with some other items. He is to record the transaction in an account (thabat). He should also look after Mūsā, who is the victim of some misfortune (mujāḥ miskīn). "As for the matter of the ships, the fear is dreadful, terrible." The letter concludes with further business matters and greetings.
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בבית המדרש לרבנים.
